description:
Goal: one revision change == one publication == one defined/update event. Signal: quicksort trace emits `semantic_closed.defined` and `materialized_program.defined` once per distinct revision, and identical replays produce no duplicate state-change events. Strategy: refactor Compiler2-owned slot maps and world define helpers so change information originates at the ownership boundary; remove unconditional emit paths for semantic closures, materialized programs, activation updates, return types, callsites, and similar stateful facts.

Change:
- Introduce a Compiler2-owned state-change result at the map/world boundary instead of returning only a revision.
- Emit `*.defined` / `*.updated` telemetry only when the underlying state actually changes.
- Tighten tests around revision/event honesty so same-state replays stay silent.

Why:
- Same-revision republishes make the trace lie and hide the real fixpoint churn.

Checks:
- Quicksort trace shows no repeated `semantic_closed.defined` or `materialized_program.defined` at the same revision.
- Activation/callsite/return telemetry reflects true state changes, not mere lookups.
title: Compiler2 signal honesty: publish and emit only on real state change
